changes.before: Not yet whatever waits at the end of this, but far enough gone.
changes.after: Already far enough gone.

changes.before: Not at the truth itself. At finally having words that matched his own warning.
changes.after: He finally had words that matched his own warning.

changes.before: Daylight made the harbor honest.
changes.after: In daylight, the harbor lay bare.

changes.before: The word *he* needed no name.
changes.after: *He* needed no name.

changes.before: The land above the harbor had forgotten softness.
changes.after: The land above the harbor had gone hard.

changes.before: Northrend enforced cold as law.
changes.after: In Northrend, the cold was absolute.

changes.before: Dwarven work announced itself even under snow.
changes.after: Even under snow, the build was plainly dwarven.

changes.before: Silence met that. Hard silence.
changes.after: No one answered at once.

changes.before: relief and suspicion refusing to give ground
changes.after: relief and suspicion set hard together on their faces

changes.before: Then his eyes went to Sylvanas, the rangers, the order in which they had entered and spread.
changes.after: Then he took in Sylvanas, the rangers, and the order in which they had entered and spread.

changes.before: His eyes found Jaina first. Then Sylvanas.
changes.after: He turned to Jaina first, then Sylvanas.

changes.before: Sylvanas looked back once toward the glacier and found, to her own surprise, that blame still refused to rise.
changes.after: Sylvanas turned once toward the glacier and found, to her own surprise, only the hard blank where blame should have been.

changes.before: The recognition went down hard and steadied her.
changes.after: She took the truth hard and steadied.

changes.before: Jaina gave her a quick, startled look, as though the word itself needed translating.
changes.after: Jaina blinked, startled.

changes.before: Jaina lifted her eyes.
changes.after: Jaina went still.

changes.before: Jaina gave her a quick, startled look
changes.after: Jaina blinked, startled.

changes.before: Jaina gave him a tired, brief smile.
changes.after: Jaina lifted a hand no higher than her cup.

changes.before: Muradin frowned.
changes.after: Muradin braced both palms on the table.

changes.before: Then Jaina gave a short nod.
changes.after: Then Jaina pushed off the table.

changes.before: The soldiers behind him turned their heads together toward the sound, not toward Muradin himself but toward the exchange, as though the blade had tugged a cord through them.
changes.after: The soldiers behind him turned their heads together toward the sound, not toward Muradin himself but toward the exchange.

changes.before: Jaina gave her a quick, startled look, as though the word itself needed translating.
changes.after: Jaina blinked, startled.

changes.before: The decisive mismatch.
changes.after: [removed]

changes.before: That, more than the fight, closed the chapter of Arthas in Sylvanas's head.
changes.after: She stopped thinking prince.

changes.before: Daylight made the harbor honest.
changes.after: In daylight, the harbor lay bare.

changes.before: The land above the harbor had forgotten softness.
changes.after: The land above the harbor had gone hard.

changes.before: The cold changed with altitude.
changes.after: Higher up, the cold cut instead of gnawing.

changes.before: The recognition went down hard and steadied her.
changes.after: She took the truth hard and steadied.

changes.before: What came next would not be another version of the same attempt.
changes.after: [removed]

changes.before: Hard silence.
changes.after: [removed]

changes.before: Triumph belonged to the scale of men. This was colder than that.
changes.after: He watched them without haste.

changes.before: Work, not panic.
changes.after: The lines matched every time.

changes.before: "The sword and the plague were made by the same kind of hand. Not the same work. Same craft."
changes.after: "The sword was forged by the same craft that made the plague."

changes.before: "I followed it from the coast because it repeats too cleanly to be natural wrongness."
changes.after: "I followed it from the coast because it turns the same way the plague does."

changes.before: "I found the strength to finish what weak men could not."
changes.after: "I found the blade to finish the campaign."

changes.before: "The sword and the plague were made by the same kind of hand. Not the same work. Same craft."
changes.after: "The sword was forged by the same craft that made the plague."
